source: replaydata/agents/opencode/scenarios/subagent-orphan-cleanup/assessment.json (1-child variant; cleanup invariant identical for any N≥1)
notes: DOCUMENTED TARGET — NOT YET RECORDED (driver_gap: restart; see assessment.json). Adapts the claudecode subagent-orphan-cleanup spec to opencode's SQLite-store transport. Parent opencode session spawns one foreground subagent via the `task` tool; once the child session row surfaces with parent_id set (transcript_new + parent_linked), the (future) recipe SIGKILLs / abruptly restarts the parent opencode process group WHILE the child turn is still in flight, leaving the child orphaned rather than reaped by its own clean exit. The daemon's pid_manager sweep must then emit process_exited for both parent and child without a spurious state_transition to working for the orphaned child during cleanup (issue #321 regression invariant). opencode is the lone ProcessOwnedStore adapter (reads session/turn state from ~/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db); the cleanup sweep reaps the rows on process exit without parsing any new store activity, so the no-working-transition invariant holds for the orphaned child. readyTTL evicts the rows after the cleanup window. Delays are looser than claudecode because opencode's session-table poll runs less frequently than fswatcher inotify notifications. The current opencode driver (drive-opencode-interactive.sh, send|wait_turn|sleep over blocking `opencode run` subprocesses) has no sigkill/restart step to orphan the child mid-turn, so this spec is the recordable target an unblocked live-REPL/process-control driver would validate.

text: User prompt drives parent ready → working
Subagent session row materializes in the SQLite store with parent_id set
SIGKILL on the parent's process group while the child turn is still in flight: pid_manager emits process_exited for the parent session

invariants: no state_transition to working for child after parent_killed
no state_transition to waiting for child after parent_killed
text: Restart sweep reaps the orphaned child without a spurious working transition (issue #321 invariant)
